scenario: An Operate trainer at Lattice Insurance walks 6 platform engineers through the official incident-resolution flow in Camunda 8.8 against a sample process with 120 instances stuck for 2 hours. The trainer wants to explain the supported variable-edit gestures and the two locations from which a retry can be initiated on a single process instance page.
question: Which combination of variable gesture and retry locations does the Operate incident-resolution flow actually support?

options.text: Edit or add a variable from the instance view, then initiate a retry from the top right corner of the page or from below the Operations heading
Edit or delete a variable from the instance view, then initiate a retry only from the top right corner of the page using the retry icon
Add a variable from the instance view, then initiate a retry from the incidents list view by selecting the affected instance row
Edit a variable from the instance view, then initiate a retry from the Modeler diagram by clicking the affected element in the BPMN canvas

a.text: Correct. The flow lets operators edit an existing variable and also add a missing variable from the instance view, then start a retry from either the top right corner of the instance page or from below the Operations heading.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs describe editing and adding variables, not deleting them, and they list two retry locations on the instance page rather than only the top right corner.

c.text: Incorrect. The retry buttons described live on the instance page itself; editing variables and starting a retry from the incidents list view is not the gesture covered in this flow.

d.text: Incorrect. Operate does not initiate retries from the Modeler canvas; retries are triggered from the process instance page in Operate, not from a BPMN editor.

explanation: When resolving an incident, Operate supports editing an existing variable or adding a missing one from the process instance view, and the instance page exposes two retry entry points: one in the top right corner and one in the Operations panel.
